claim: Expert-rubric inter-judge agreement was reported as mean pairwise quadratic-weighted kappa 0.60.
scope: 92 Figma runs reported in rubric comparison
caveat: Comparison changed author source rubric count grouping weights anchor language and observability together
decision_use: Supports testing skill-grounded scoring for judge consistency; does not identify which bundled rubric feature produced the difference.

claim: Generic-rubric inter-judge agreement was reported as mean pairwise quadratic-weighted kappa 0.46.
caveat: Does not isolate expert authorship as the cause
decision_use: Provides the comparison point supporting a controlled test rather than attributing the agreement gap to expert authorship.

claim: Expert-rubric judge variance was reported as mean 0.10.
decision_use: Supports a consistency benefit while explicitly withholding a validity conclusion.

claim: Generic-rubric judge variance was reported as mean 0.25.
decision_use: Provides disconfirming context for generic-rubric consistency, but cannot establish professional validity for the alternative.

claim: Human/LLM concordance on individual outputs was reported as kappa 0.08.
caveat: One Figma domain expert limits human-baseline generality
decision_use: Disconfirms treating improved inter-judge agreement as sufficient evidence of fine-grained criterion validity.

claim: Human/LLM concordance on individual skills was reported as kappa 0.06.
caveat: No independent human process annotation validates the skill judges
decision_use: Disconfirms current readiness for unmonitored skill-level scoring and motivates independent process annotation in a pilot.

claim: The skill execution ablation comprised 7 paired runs over 3 tasks and found directional, heterogeneous effects.
scope: Claude Codex and Gemini harness/model systems
caveat: Sample is underpowered and harness/model/integration factors remain bundled
decision_use: Supports a controlled, adequately powered replication; does not support a stable causal adoption claim.

claim: One configured Codex system had an aggregate skill delta of +0.87.
scope: Subset of the 7 paired Figma runs
caveat: Too few runs for a stable causal adoption claim
decision_use: Treat as a promising directional signal for pilot sizing, not as a general causal effect.

claim: Deployment occurred in 2 of 7 no-skill runs.
caveat: Deployment difference alone cannot separate procedure content from integration and stochasticity
decision_use: Supports measuring completion and deployment while holding integration fixed; does not identify procedure content as the cause.

claim: 60 percent of pairwise-winning outputs met the expert >=4 readiness threshold.
scope: 31 rated Figma tasks with one domain expert
caveat: Single-expert threshold is not a universal professional standard
decision_use: Disconfirms equating relative wins with absolute readiness and requires a preregistered multi-expert readiness criterion.
